pages.full_text:
We had an uneventful tunnel crossing, thank goodness, & reached Charles at 3 as predicted. But unfortunately as Simon reversed into the space outside their house in Frenchay Rd. there was a crunch. The woman who drove up behind didn't realise we were reversing.

     Charles & Jenny were well & so was Tinder. We took him on a walk to photograph the scratch on the woman's car (it wasn't there so Charles photographed it later). & then reached Westfield Rd at 6.30. A super, long holiday.

__June 2nd Friday__
8.45. Conrad, Eleni, Tinder, Simon & I set off for Telford where Simon met with Chris Sparks to discuss conveyor belts with [?Tamer] for the hop barn for 1½ hours. On to Berrington Hall NT while we napped. There
pages.notes: The word before 'for the hop barn' could read 'Tamer' or 'Jamer' — unclear. The previous paragraph (ending '...A super, long holiday') appears to conclude an earlier trip entry. 'June 2nd Friday' matches 2017-06-02; this is the most likely year given volume range 2003-2019.

pages.full_text:
Tinder went wild straining at the sheep until we got into the grounds. The children played in the playground & we had cake in the courtyard & then into the shop/cafe when it rained. Tinder sat under the table umbrella.

     On to the house Peter has rented for Hay festival w/end. It sleeps 5. Charles & Jenny, John & Kay came, but John & Kay 6ft straight away because Kay had a stiff shoulder. land lady nice but fussy. Sends the van & S, C, E & E & T away to sleep in a farmer's field. Peter had supper with us & then the children wanted to sleep at Peter's house, so at 8.45. he took them there, then brought them back at 9.45. because the land lady has a mezzanine floor & worries about children. (When we cdn't find her via postcode, Simon had said "all we saw was dilapidated farm buildings"). Then we all went to sleep, phew! & the chil. slept till 7.10.

__Sat__ Peter & Charles arrived for an outdoor leisurely breakfast. Then Peter & Simon & Eve set off for the Hay Book festival (30 years) & parked in a field (£3). We wandered in the
pages.notes: Text continues off the bottom of the page. 'Sat' heading is underlined. The 'Hay Book festival (30 years)' reference helps confirm approximate date — the Hay Festival celebrated its 30th anniversary in 2017, further supporting the 2017 date. 'cdn't' is the diary's own abbreviation for 'couldn't'.
